title: Block 1 Test: The Logic Gate (Conditionals)
learningGoal: Comprehensively evaluate the learner's ability to construct, translate, and distinguish between real and unreal conditional clauses.
instructions: This test evaluates your mastery over 'Agar... toh...' and hypothetical sentence structures. Pay extreme attention to the verb endings (future vs cutoff).

questions.questionText: Translate: 'If you go, then I will come.'
options: Agar tum jaoge, toh main aaunga.
Agar tum jaate, toh main aata.

questionHints.targetText: Agar tum jaoge, toh main aaunga.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank: 'Jaldi karo, _____ train chhoot jayegi.' (Hurry up, otherwise the train will be missed).
Translate: 'If I were a doctor, I would help.'
options: Agar main doctor hota, toh main madad karta.
Agar main doctor hota hoon, toh main madad karunga.
Agar main doctor tha, toh main madad karta.

questionHints.targetText: Agar main doctor hota, toh main madad karta.

questions.questionText: Translate to English: काश मेरे पास गाड़ी होती! (Kaash mere paas gaadi hoti!)

questionHints.targetText: I wish I had a car!

questions.questionText: What makes a sentence a 'Type 2 Unreal' conditional in Hindi?
options: Dropping 'hai' or 'tha' at the end of the verb, leaving just the 'ta/ti/te' suffix.
Using the past tense 'tha' at the very end.

questionHints.targetText: Dropping 'hai' or 'tha' at the end of the verb, leaving just the 'ta/ti/te' suffix.

answerKey.tq_1: Agar tum jaoge, toh main aaunga.
answerKey.tq_3: Agar main doctor hota, toh main madad karta.
answerKey.tq_4: I wish I had a car!
answerKey.tq_5: Dropping 'hai' or 'tha' at the end of the verb, leaving just the 'ta/ti/te' suffix.

partialCreditRules.tq_4: Accept 'I wish I had my own car'.

feedbackRules.onPass: Legendary! You understand exactly how to manipulate reality using Hindi verbs.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the core rule of hypotheticals: Never end with 'tha'. Tha means something actually happened in the past. We want to talk about imagination.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. Remember that 'Warna' means 'Otherwise', which is like a shortcut conditional.

hintText: Read the options for Q3 carefully. If you use 'tha', you are saying 'If I WAS a doctor (in 1999)', not 'If I WERE a doctor (right now)'.
commonMistakes: Choosing option 3 for Q1, which drops the word 'toh'. You can drop 'agar', but you can NEVER drop 'toh'.
